content.s1_claims_discipline_internal:
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# TAGS: lab analysis engine, workflow, orchestrator, lab processing, analysis pipeline, interpret labs, analyze blood test, run analysis, full analysis, comprehensive analysis, step by step, lab workflow, biomarker processing, calculate ratios, pattern recognition, dossier generation, how to analyze, lab report, lab PDF, process labs, intake labs, new labs, patient labs, run the numbers, full workup, analysis engine, lab pipeline, marker scan, ratio calculation, terrain scan, system analysis, mechanistic model, probability hierarchy, FBCA workflow, dossier workflow, analysis checklist, quality control, completeness check, missing markers, assay verification, cross-system patterns, coupled analysis, integrated interpretation
# CATEGORY: Clinical Tools
# USE FOR: Processing any new set of lab results from intake to finished analysis/dossier. This is the MASTER WORKFLOW that orchestrates all other clinical tools.
# COUPLED_SYSTEMS: 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS, 35_LAB_INTERPRETATION, 61_TERRAIN_ASSESSMENT, 62_CLINICAL_REASONING_ANOMALIES, 64_DOSSIER_PROTOCOL, 36_TESTING_GUIDE, 06_METABOLIC, 09_HPA_ADRENAL, 17_MALE_HORMONES, 18_FEMALE_HORMONES, 19_CARDIOVASCULAR, 57_THYROID_COMPLETE, 63_SPORT_PERFORMANCE_CONTEXT
# RED_FLAGS: Never skip terrain scan. Never skip anomaly check. Never deliver analysis without confidence grading. Never skip assay method verification for hormones.
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# LAB ANALYSIS ENGINE — MASTER ORCHESTRATOR
# From Raw Values to Finished Clinical Analysis
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
#
# PURPOSE:
# This file is the PROCEDURAL BACKBONE of Valentine's lab analysis capability.
# It ensures that every analysis — whether a quick 10-marker check or a full
# 75-marker comprehensive panel — follows the same rigorous, reproducible
# workflow. No marker is missed. No ratio is forgotten. No pattern is ignored.
#
# RELATIONSHIP TO OTHER FILES:
# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
# │  66_LAB_ANALYSIS_ENGINE (this file) = HOW (procedural workflow)    │
# │  65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS                 = WHAT (individual markers)    │
# │  35_LAB_INTERPRETATION              = MATH (ratios & patterns)     │
# │  61_TERRAIN_ASSESSMENT              = GATE (ready or not)          │
# │  62_CLINICAL_REASONING_ANOMALIES    = FLAG (unexpected values)     │
# │  64_DOSSIER_PROTOCOL                = FORMAT (output structure)    │
# │  System files (06,09,17,18,19,57…)  = WHY (mechanistic depth)     │
# │  36_TESTING_GUIDE                   = NEXT (what's missing)        │
# └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
#
# WHEN TO USE THIS FILE:
# - ANY time Valentine receives lab results (PDF, photo, typed values)
# - ANY time Valentine is asked to "analyze," "interpret," or "review" labs
# - ANY time a dossier/FBCA/report is requested
# - ANY time labs are uploaded for a new or existing patient
#
# This file is NOT for single-marker questions ("what does my TSH mean?")
# — those route directly to 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS. This file activates when
# Valentine has a PANEL of results to process systematically.
#
# ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


# ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐

 === SECTION 1: CLAIMS DISCIPLINE [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## HOW WE ANALYZE YOUR LAB RESULTS

When you share your lab results with us, we don't just check if values are
"normal" or "abnormal." We run a comprehensive 9-step analysis that looks
at each marker individually, calculates ratios between markers, identifies
patterns across body systems, and builds a complete picture of your health.

This process is the same whether you're a new patient or returning for
follow-up. Consistency matters — it means nothing gets missed, and we can
accurately track changes over time.

### OUR ANALYSIS INCLUDES:

**Individual Marker Assessment** — Each biomarker is evaluated against
functional optimal ranges, not just standard lab reference ranges. A value
can be "normal" by lab standards but suboptimal for your health.

**Calculated Ratios & Indices** — We calculate 15+ ratios that reveal
patterns invisible when looking at individual markers alone. For example,
your triglyceride-to-glucose index reveals insulin resistance years before
fasting glucose becomes abnormal.

**Cross-System Pattern Recognition** — Your body is an integrated system.
We look at how markers from different systems interact — thyroid affecting
cholesterol, inflammation affecting iron readings, stress hormones affecting
metabolic markers.

**Terrain Assessment** — Before recommending any treatment, we assess
whether your body is ready. This prevents wasting resources on advanced
therapies when foundational issues need attention first.

**Mechanistic Explanation** — We explain WHY your values are what they are,
not just WHAT they are. Understanding the mechanism helps you make informed
decisions about your health.

---

 === SECTION 2: PATIENT COMMUNICATION [PATIENT_OK] ===
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## MASTER WORKFLOW — 9 STEPS
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
##
## OVERVIEW:
##
##   STEP 1: INTAKE & EXTRACTION ──────── Get the raw data clean
##   STEP 2: CONTEXT CAPTURE ──────────── Who is this patient, what context
##   STEP 3: INDIVIDUAL MARKER SCAN ───── Each marker through 65_ATLAS
##   STEP 4: CALCULATED RATIOS ────────── All applicable ratios via 35_LAB
##   STEP 5: ANOMALY DETECTION ────────── 62_CLINICAL_REASONING scan
##   STEP 6: TERRAIN ASSESSMENT ───────── 61_TERRAIN 4-layer gate
##   STEP 7: CROSS-SYSTEM SYNTHESIS ───── Pattern recognition & coupling
##   STEP 8: MECHANISTIC MODEL ────────── Probability hierarchy, hypotheses
##   STEP 9: OUTPUT GENERATION ────────── Dossier via 64_DOSSIER_PROTOCOL
##
## Each step produces a defined output that feeds the next step.
## Steps 3-6 can run in parallel conceptually, but all must complete
## before Step 7 begins.
##
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════


## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## STEP 1: INTAKE & EXTRACTION
## Source: Lab PDF / photo / typed values
## Output: Clean structured marker list with units and methods
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 1.1 IDENTIFY LAB SOURCE

Record:
- Lab provider name (Prodia, BioReference, Quest, Omni, etc.)
- Date of blood draw (NOT date of report)
- Fasting status if stated
- Time of draw if stated (critical for: cortisol AM, testosterone, insulin)

### 1.2 EXTRACT ALL VALUES

Create a structured list:
- Marker name (standardize to KB naming: "FBG" not "Glucose, Fasting")
- Value
- Unit (flag if non-standard unit — convert to KB standard units)
- Lab reference range (record but DO NOT use for interpretation)
- Lab flag (H/L/Normal — record but Valentine uses its own 7-tier system)

### 1.3 ASSAY METHOD VERIFICATION

MANDATORY for these markers — do NOT proceed to interpretation without this:

| Marker | Risk | What to Check | If Unknown |
|--------|------|---------------|------------|
| Free Testosterone | Direct vs Calculated = 5x scale difference | Lab report method line, or unit clue (pg/mL vs ng/dL) | State assumption explicitly: "Method not specified on report — assuming [X] based on [value range / lab provider default]" |
| Cortisol | AM single snapshot vs 4-point vs DUTCH | Time of draw, test name | If AM draw: flag as "single AM snapshot — screening only, not diagnostic" (→ 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS: Cortisol AM) |
| Estradiol | Standard vs Sensitive (LC-MS/MS) | Test name, method line | Sensitive required for male patients. If standard: flag "standard assay — less accurate at low male ranges" |
| HbA1c | Hemoglobin variants can interfere | Patient ethnicity, known hemoglobinopathy | If Hb variant suspected: flag "HbA1c may be falsely low/high" (→ 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS: HbA1c confounders) |
| Vitamin D | 25-OH total vs D2 vs D3 separately | Test name | If only D2 or D3: flag "total 25-OH-D preferred for clinical decisions" |

### 1.4 UNIT STANDARDIZATION

Convert all values to KB standard units:
| Marker Category | KB Standard Unit |
|----------------|-----------------|
| Glucose | mg/dL (× 18.02 if mmol/L) |
| Cholesterol, LDL, HDL | mg/dL (× 38.67 if mmol/L) |
| Triglycerides | mg/dL (× 88.57 if mmol/L) |
| Creatinine | mg/dL (÷ 88.4 if µmol/L) |
| BUN | mg/dL (÷ 0.357 if mmol/L urea) |
| Testosterone (total) | ng/dL |
| Free Testosterone | pg/mL (direct) or ng/dL (calculated) — SPECIFY WHICH |
| Estradiol | pg/mL |
| Cortisol | µg/dL (÷ 27.59 if nmol/L) |
| TSH | mIU/L |
| Free T4 | ng/dL |
| Free T3 | pg/mL |
| Vitamin D | ng/mL (÷ 2.496 if nmol/L) |
| Ferritin | ng/mL |
| Hemoglobin | g/dL |
| hsCRP | mg/L |
| Insulin | µIU/mL |
| Homocysteine | µmol/L |

### 1.5 INVENTORY & GAP IDENTIFICATION

Count total markers available. Classify panel completeness:

| Panel Level | Markers Available | Capability |
|-------------|-------------------|------------|
| MINIMAL | <15 markers (basic CBC + CMP only) | Individual marker assessment only. Limited ratios. Flag major gaps. |
| STANDARD | 15-35 markers (CBC + CMP + lipids + thyroid) | Full individual scan + core ratios. Some patterns possible. |
| COMPREHENSIVE | 35-55 markers (+ hormones + inflammation + iron) | Full individual + all ratios + pattern recognition + terrain. |
| ELITE | 55+ markers (+ ApoB + Lp(a) + insulin + specialized) | Complete analysis. All ratios. All patterns. Full mechanistic model. |

Flag critical missing markers using this checklist:

| Marker | When to Flag as Missing | Impact of Absence |
|--------|------------------------|-------------------|
| ApoB | ALWAYS when LDL-C >100 | Cannot finalize cardiovascular risk. Incomplete lipid analysis. |
| Lp(a) | ALWAYS (test once, genetic) | Cannot assess genetic CVD risk modifier. |
| Fasting Insulin | When FBG >85 or HbA1c >5.3 or TG >100 or TG:HDL >2 | Cannot calculate HOMA-IR or TyG. Misses early IR. |
| FT3 + rT3 | When TSH >2.0 or FT4 borderline | Cannot assess conversion. TSH alone incomplete. |
| DHEA-S | When cortisol AM <15 | Cannot assess adrenal reserve. Cortisol alone incomplete. |
| Ferritin + TIBC | When Hb <13 M / <12 F or MCV abnormal | Cannot differentiate iron deficiency type. |
| Vitamin D | ALWAYS | Foundational marker — affects immune, bone, hormones, mood. |
| Magnesium | When GGT <10 or arrhythmia symptoms or cramps | Serum Mg reflects only 1% of total. RBC Mg preferred. |
| hsCRP | ALWAYS for cardiovascular or inflammation assessment | Cannot distinguish inflammatory vs non-inflammatory pathology. |
| Homocysteine | When B12 normal but neurological symptoms, or NAD+ candidate | Functional methylation marker. |

**RULE: Every missing critical marker must appear in the final output's
"Recommended Additional Testing" section with rationale for WHY it's needed.**

→ Output of Step 1: Clean structured marker list, assay methods documented,
   panel level classified, gaps identified.


## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## STEP 2: CONTEXT CAPTURE
## Source: Patient history, intake, prior labs, conversation
## Output: Context profile that modifies interpretation
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 2.1 PATIENT DEMOGRAPHICS

Record:
- Age (adjusts: eGFR, DHEA-S, testosterone, bone markers)
- Sex (adjusts: Hb, ferritin, creatinine, hormones, iron)
- Known conditions (adjusts: interpretation of all markers)
- Current medications / supplements (adjusts: confounders)

### 2.2 CONTEXT MODIFIERS

Check each — any YES changes interpretation logic:

| Context | If YES → Impact | KB Reference |
|---------|-----------------|-------------|
| Athlete / heavy training | CPK up to 500 expected. Creatinine may be elevated (muscular). Hct may be higher. | 63_SPORT_PERFORMANCE_CONTEXT |
| On TRT / AAS | Expect elevated total T, suppressed LH/FSH, watch Hct >50%, E2 may be high | 63_SPORT_PERFORMANCE_CONTEXT, 17_MALE_HORMONES |
| On thyroid medication | TSH may be suppressed by design. Evaluate FT3/FT4, not TSH alone. | 57_THYROID_COMPLETE |
| On statin | LDL will be pharmacologically lowered. ApoB more relevant. CPK may be elevated. | 19_CARDIOVASCULAR |
| On PPI / metformin | B12 depletion risk. Mg depletion risk. Homocysteine may rise. | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS: B12, Mg |
| On oral contraceptive (F) | SHBG elevated → total T may be normal but Free T low. Lipids shifted. | 18_FEMALE_HORMONES |
| Pregnant (F) | Completely different reference ranges. Route to 28_PREGNANCY. | 28_PREGNANCY |
| Post-menopausal (F) | Hormone ranges shift. Lipids naturally rise. Bone markers critical. | 18_FEMALE_HORMONES |
| Tropical residence (Bali) | Eosinophils >0.5 consider parasites. UV exposure affects VitD interpretation. Dengue can affect platelets/WBC. | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS: Eosinophils, VitD |
| Recent illness / surgery | Acute phase reactants elevated (ferritin, hsCRP, platelets, WBC). Delay interpretation 4-6 weeks. | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS: Ferritin, hsCRP |
| Fasting state unknown | FBG, insulin, TG less reliable. Flag but still analyze. | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS: FBG, Insulin |
| Time of draw unknown | Cortisol AM and testosterone less reliable if not AM draw. Flag. | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS: Cortisol, Total T |

### 2.3 PRIOR LABS COMPARISON

If prior labs are available:
- Flag direction of change for every marker (↑ improving, ↓ worsening, → stable)
- Calculate rate of change for critical markers (eGFR decline rate, HbA1c trend)
- Note protocol that was in place between labs
- Assess protocol efficacy: did the intervention move the expected marker?

→ Output of Step 2: Context profile — demographics, modifiers, prior comparison.


## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## STEP 3: INDIVIDUAL MARKER SCAN
## Source: Clean marker list (Step 1) → 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS
## Output: 7-tier classification for every marker + flags
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 3.1 CLASSIFY EVERY MARKER

For EACH marker in the patient's labs:

1. Look up in 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS
2. Apply 7-tier range: CRITICALLY LOW → LOW → SUBOPTIMAL → OPTIMAL → SUPRA-OPTIMAL → HIGH → CRITICALLY HIGH
3. Apply context modifiers from Step 2 (athlete, medication, age, sex)
4. Record tier + any confounders that might shift interpretation
5. Flag markers at CRITICALLY LOW, CRITICALLY HIGH, or any value >2x upper limit

### 3.2 MANDATORY SCAN TABLE FORMAT

Build internal table (not for patient — this is Valentine's working document):

| # | Marker | Value | Unit | 7-Tier | Confounders | Note |
|---|--------|-------|------|--------|-------------|------|

Example:
| 1 | FBG | 89 | mg/dL | OPTIMAL | Fasted confirmed | |
| 2 | HbA1c | 5.4 | % | OPTIMAL | No hemoglobin variant | |
| 3 | Insulin | 5.9 | µIU/mL | OPTIMAL | Fasted confirmed | |
| 4 | TSH | 2.1 | mIU/L | SUPRA-OPTIMAL | On no thyroid meds | Consider FT3/rT3 |
| 5 | FT4 | 0.87 | ng/dL | SUBOPTIMAL | | Check FT3 |

### 3.3 FLAG SUMMARY

After scanning all markers, generate:
- Count by tier: X optimal, Y suboptimal, Z low, etc.
- List of all non-optimal markers (these become candidates for ratio analysis)
- List of critically flagged markers (these get priority in synthesis)

→ Output of Step 3: Complete 7-tier scan table + tier summary + flag list.


## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## STEP 4: CALCULATED RATIOS & DERIVED INDICES
## Source: Raw values (Step 1) → 35_LAB_INTERPRETATION Section 2
## Output: All applicable ratios calculated and classified
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 4.1 MANDATORY RATIO CHECKLIST

Calculate EVERY ratio for which the required inputs are available.
Do NOT skip ratios because the individual markers look normal — ratios
reveal patterns that individual values miss.

| Ratio/Index | Formula | Required Inputs | When to Calculate |
|-------------|---------|-----------------|-------------------|
| **METABOLIC** | | | |
| HOMA-IR | (FBG × Insulin) ÷ 405 | FBG + Fasting Insulin | ALWAYS when both available |
| TyG Index | Ln(TG × FBG ÷ 2) | TG + FBG | ALWAYS when both available |
| eAG | (28.7 × HbA1c) − 46.7 | HbA1c | ALWAYS when available |
| TG:HDL | TG ÷ HDL | TG + HDL | ALWAYS when both available |
| **CARDIOVASCULAR** | | | |
| TC:HDL | TC ÷ HDL | TC + HDL | ALWAYS when both available |
| LDL:HDL | LDL ÷ HDL | LDL + HDL | ALWAYS when both available |
| AIP | Log10(TG ÷ HDL) | TG + HDL (mg/dL) | ALWAYS when both available |
| Non-HDL-C | TC − HDL | TC + HDL | ALWAYS when both available |
| **INFLAMMATION** | | | |
| NLR | Neutrophils ÷ Lymphocytes | Absolute Neut + Lymph | ALWAYS when CBC with diff available |
| PLR | Platelets ÷ Lymphocytes (×1000) | Platelets + Lymph | ALWAYS when CBC with diff available |
| **LIVER** | | | |
| AST:ALT | AST ÷ ALT | AST + ALT | ALWAYS when both available |
| A:G Ratio | Albumin ÷ Globulin | Albumin + Globulin (or Total Protein) | ALWAYS when available |
| **KIDNEY** | | | |
| BUN:Creatinine | BUN ÷ Creatinine | BUN + Creatinine | ALWAYS when both available |
| **MINERAL / HYDRATION** | | | |
| Ca:P | Calcium ÷ Phosphorus | Ca + P | ALWAYS when both available |
| Na:K | Sodium ÷ Potassium | Na + K | ALWAYS when both available |
| Calculated Osmolality | (2 × Na) + (BUN ÷ 2.8) + (Glucose ÷ 18) | Na + BUN + Glucose | When electrolytes available |
| Corrected Calcium | Ca + 0.8 × (4.0 − Albumin) | Ca + Albumin | ALWAYS when Albumin <4.0 |
| **HORMONAL** | | | |
| Cortisol:DHEA-S | Cortisol ÷ (DHEA-S ÷ 100) | Cortisol + DHEA-S | When adrenal axis evaluated |
| T:E2 (male) | Total T ÷ E2 | Total T + E2 | ALWAYS for male patients |
| Free T % | (Free T ÷ Total T) × 100 | Free T + Total T | ALWAYS when both available |
| rT3:FT3 | rT3 ÷ FT3 | rT3 + FT3 | When thyroid conversion evaluated |
| **IRON** | | | |
| % Transferrin Sat | (Iron ÷ TIBC) × 100 | Iron + TIBC | ALWAYS when both available |
| **HEMATOLOGY** | | | |
| Mentzer Index | MCV ÷ RBC | MCV + RBC | When microcytic anemia: >13 = iron deficiency, <13 = thalassemia trait |

### 4.2 CLASSIFY EACH RATIO

For each calculated ratio, apply 7-tier classification from 35_LAB_INTERPRETATION.
Record in working table alongside individual markers.

### 4.3 RATIO RED FLAGS

These ratio results demand immediate attention:

| Ratio | Critical Value | Implication |
|-------|---------------|-------------|
| HOMA-IR | >5.0 | Severe insulin resistance — metabolic terrain RED |
| TyG | >9.0 | High metabolic risk — cross-check with fasting status |
| NLR | >6.0 | Significant inflammatory stress — rule out infection/malignancy |
| AST:ALT | >2.0 | Alcoholic hepatitis pattern or advanced liver disease |
| BUN:Cr | >20 | Pre-renal azotemia (dehydration) vs GI bleed |
| Na:K | >38 | Aldosterone excess pattern — investigate |
| % Transferrin Sat | >45% | Iron overload risk — check hemochromatosis |
| % Transferrin Sat | <16% | Functional iron deficiency — even if ferritin "normal" |
| Hematocrit | >50% (on TRT) | Dose reduction required. >52% phlebotomy. >54% STOP TRT. |

→ Output of Step 4: Complete ratio table with 7-tier classifications + ratio red flags.


## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## STEP 5: ANOMALY DETECTION
## Source: All markers + ratios → 62_CLINICAL_REASONING_ANOMALIES
## Output: Anomaly flags with differential considerations
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 5.1 SCAN FOR ANOMALIES

Run the 5 override triggers from 62_CLINICAL_REASONING_ANOMALIES:

| Trigger | Check | Action |
|---------|-------|--------|
| TRIGGER 1 | Any value >2x upper limit of normal | Stop template. Investigate individually. |
| TRIGGER 2 | Patient on exogenous substances that shift terrain | Adjust ALL related markers before interpreting. |
| TRIGGER 3 | Lab combination suggests specific diagnosis | Flag diagnosis for consideration (not confirmation). |
| TRIGGER 4 | Previous labs available for comparison | Calculate delta. Flag rapid changes. |
| TRIGGER 5 | Patient age changes interpretation | Apply age-adjusted ranges (DHEA-S, testosterone, eGFR). |

### 5.2 DISCORDANCE CHECK

Look for values that don't make sense together:

| Discordance | Pattern | Most Likely Explanation |
|-------------|---------|----------------------|
| LDL-C normal + ApoB high | Particle discordance | Small dense LDL — ApoB wins, higher risk than LDL suggests |
| LDL-C high + ApoB normal | Particle concordance favorable | Large buoyant LDL — lower risk than LDL suggests |
| Ferritin high + Iron low | Inflammatory ferritin | hsCRP should be elevated — ferritin is acute phase, not iron storage here |
| TSH normal + FT4 low | Central hypothyroidism pattern | Pituitary issue — TSH should be high if thyroid is underperforming |
| Cortisol high + DHEA-S low | Chronic stress pattern | HPA axis prioritizing cortisol over DHEA — adrenal steal |
| HbA1c normal + Insulin high | Early insulin resistance | Pancreas compensating — HbA1c will rise LATER if unchecked |
| MCV high + B12 normal | Functional B12 deficiency | Check MMA (methylmalonic acid) — serum B12 misses functional deficit |
| GGT low + MCHC low + RDW high | Micronutrient depletion | B6 + Mg + early iron insufficiency — cofactor triad |
| Total T high + Free T low | SHBG sequestration | SHBG binding testosterone — fix SHBG drivers, not testosterone |
| eGFR low + Creatinine normal-high | Muscular patient | Cystatin C needed for accurate GFR in muscular individuals |

### 5.3 SPORT / TRT CONTEXT CHECK

If patient is athlete, bodybuilder, or on TRT/AAS:
→ Route to 63_SPORT_PERFORMANCE_CONTEXT BEFORE interpreting:
- CPK (up to 500 post-training is expected)
- Hematocrit (TRT monitoring protocol)
- LH/FSH (expected suppressed on TRT)
- Liver enzymes (training can elevate AST > ALT)
- Testosterone, E2 (exogenous ranges differ from physiological)

→ Output of Step 5: Anomaly flags, discordance pairs, sport context adjustments.


## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## STEP 6: TERRAIN ASSESSMENT
## Source: All markers → 61_TERRAIN_ASSESSMENT
## Output: 4-layer terrain status (GREEN / YELLOW / RED per layer)
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 6.1 RUN FULL TERRAIN SCAN

Execute ALL 4 layers from 61_TERRAIN_ASSESSMENT:

| Layer | Parameters | GREEN | YELLOW | RED |
|-------|-----------|-------|--------|-----|
| 1. HORMONAL | Cortisol AM, FT3/rT3, Free T, Estradiol, DHEA-S | All in functional range | 1-2 borderline | Any critical or conversion blocked |
| 2. INFLAMMATORY | hsCRP, NLR, Ferritin context, ESR | hsCRP <1, NLR <2 | hsCRP 1-3, NLR 2-3 | hsCRP >3, NLR >3 |
| 3. COFACTORS & O2 | Hb, Ferritin, B12, VitD, Mg, Iron sat | All adequate | 1-2 borderline low | Hb <12F/<13M, Ferritin <20, VitD <20 |
| 4. GUT / DETOX | GGT, ALT, Bilirubin, Albumin | All normal | GGT or ALT mildly elevated | GGT >3x or ALT >2x or Albumin <3.5 |

### 6.2 TERRAIN GATING RULES

The terrain status GATES what can go in Phase 1 (Begin Now) of any protocol:

| Terrain Status | Phase 1 Allowed | Phase 2 Only |
|----------------|----------------|-------------|
| ALL GREEN | Full protocol: lifestyle + supplements + IV + advanced | N/A |
| ANY YELLOW | Lifestyle + oral supplements + low-risk IV (hydration, B-complex) | NAD+ IV, intensive peptides, aggressive hormone protocols |
| ANY RED | Lifestyle modifications ONLY + targeted correction of RED parameter | Everything else — gated behind terrain correction |

### 6.3 TERRAIN SUMMARY TABLE

Build (for internal use and dossier inclusion):

| Layer | Status | Key Drivers | Gate Impact |
|-------|--------|------------|-------------|
| Hormonal | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | [specific markers] | [what's gated] |
| Inflammatory | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | [specific markers] | [what's gated] |
| Cofactors & O2 | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | [specific markers] | [what's gated] |
| Gut / Detox | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | [specific markers] | [what's gated] |
| **OVERALL** | **[worst layer]** | | |

**CRITICAL RULE: Overall terrain = worst individual layer.**
One RED layer = overall RED, regardless of other layers.

→ Output of Step 6: 4-layer terrain table + gating implications.


## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## STEP 7: CROSS-SYSTEM SYNTHESIS
## Source: Steps 3-6 outputs → 35_LAB_INTERPRETATION patterns + system files
## Output: Identified axes with coupled-system connections
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 7.1 PATTERN RECOGNITION

Scan for the 6 cross-system pattern combinations from 35_LAB_INTERPRETATION:

| Pattern | Markers Involved | If Present → |
|---------|-----------------|-------------|
| Metabolic-Inflammatory | NLR ↑ + TyG ↑ + hsCRP ↑ | Insulin resistance driving inflammation. 06_METABOLIC primary. |
| Hormonal-Thyroid-Clearance | TC:HDL ↑ + TG:HDL ↑ + SHBG abnormal + Free T abnormal | Thyroid affecting lipid clearance + hormonal terrain. 57_THYROID + 19_CV. |
| Stress-Depletion | Cortisol:DHEA ↑ + Na:K ↑ + GGT ↓ + Mg low | Chronic stress depleting cofactors. 09_HPA primary. |
| Intracellular Dehydration | Osmolality ↑ + Cl low + Mg low + Na borderline high | Cellular dehydration. 39_HYDRATION. |
| Aromatization (male) | T:E2 ↓ + SHBG ↓ + body fat elevated + E2 ↑ | Excess aromatase activity. 17_MALE_HORMONES. |
| Occult Anemia | MCHC ↓ + RDW ↑ + GGT ↓ + Total Protein ↓ | Early micronutrient insufficiency. 65_ATLAS iron + cofactors. |

### 7.2 IDENTIFY AFFECTED AXES

Group non-optimal findings into system axes:

| Axis | Markers in This Axis | Status | Primary KB File |
|------|---------------------|--------|----------------|
| Metabolic | FBG, HbA1c, Insulin, HOMA-IR, TyG | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 06_METABOLIC |
| Thyroid | TSH, FT4, FT3, rT3, rT3:FT3 | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 57_THYROID_COMPLETE |
| Adrenal | Cortisol, DHEA-S, Cortisol:DHEA | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 09_HPA_ADRENAL |
| Cardiovascular | TC, LDL, HDL, TG, ApoB, Lp(a), hsCRP, AIP | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 19_CARDIOVASCULAR |
| Hormonal M | Total T, Free T, E2, SHBG, T:E2 | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 17_MALE_HORMONES |
| Hormonal F | E2, P4, FSH, LH, SHBG (+ cycle phase) | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 18_FEMALE_HORMONES |
| Hematological | RBC, Hb, Hct, MCV, MCH, MCHC, RDW, WBC diff | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS |
| Iron | Iron, Ferritin, TIBC, %Sat | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS |
| Liver | AST, ALT, GGT, ALP, Bilirubin, AST:ALT | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS |
| Kidney | BUN, Creatinine, eGFR, Uric Acid | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS |
| Electrolytes | Na, K, Cl, CO2, Ca, P, Mg, Ca:P, Na:K | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS |
| Vitamins | VitD, B12, Folate | GREEN/YELLOW/RED | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS |

### 7.3 MAP CONNECTIONS BETWEEN AXES

For every non-GREEN axis, identify connections to other non-GREEN axes:

Example connection mapping:
- Thyroid YELLOW (TSH 2.8, FT4 low-normal) → CV YELLOW (LDL 135)
  Connection: TSH >2.5 reduces LDL receptor expression → impaired LDL clearance
  KB source: 19_CARDIOVASCULAR thyroid-lipid framework
  Implication: Fix thyroid FIRST, then reassess LDL before lipid intervention

- Adrenal YELLOW (Cortisol 11, DHEA-S low) → Metabolic YELLOW (FBG 95)
  Connection: Cortisol dysregulation → elevated dawn glucose → cortisol-driven gluconeogenesis
  KB source: 09_HPA_ADRENAL, 06_METABOLIC, 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS FBG cortisol dawn effect
  Implication: Elevated FBG may be adrenal, not metabolic. TyG index differentiates.

**RULE: Never interpret an axis in isolation when another connected axis
is also non-GREEN. The connection must be stated in the output.**

→ Output of Step 7: Identified axes with status, pattern matches, connection map.


## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## STEP 8: MECHANISTIC MODEL & PROBABILITY HIERARCHY
## Source: Step 7 synthesis → Valentine diagnostic reasoning (prompt §6)
## Output: Leading hypothesis, alternatives, falsifiability
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 8.1 BUILD MECHANISTIC MODEL

For the patient's complete picture, construct:

**Clinical Thesis (one sentence):**
"The most biologically plausible model is that [upstream driver] is causing
[downstream effects] via [mechanism], manifesting as [observed pattern]."

Example: "The most biologically plausible model is that subclinical thyroid
underconversion (FT4 0.87, pending FT3 confirmation) is contributing to
impaired LDL clearance (LDL 115) and suboptimal energy metabolism, in a
patient with otherwise excellent metabolic and hormonal terrain."

### 8.2 PROBABILITY HIERARCHY (per axis)

For EACH non-GREEN axis, apply:

| Element | Content | Confidence |
|---------|---------|-----------|
| MOST LIKELY | "The most biologically plausible explanation for [markers] is..." | HIGH / MOD-HIGH / MODERATE / LOW |
| ALTERNATIVE | "A plausible alternative is..." | With rationale |
| DANGEROUS EXCLUSION | "Unlikely but must exclude..." | Only if applicable — do not force |
| PROBABILITY SHIFT | "If [test result], probability shifts to [alternative]..." | Specific test + specific threshold |
| FALSIFICATION | "This interpretation is WRONG if [condition]..." | Concrete and testable |

### 8.3 CONFIDENCE GRADING

Grade each finding:

| Grade | Criteria | Example |
|-------|---------|---------|
| HIGH | ≥3 converging biomarkers + established mechanism + no confounders | HOMA-IR 4.2 + TyG 9.1 + TG:HDL 3.5 = insulin resistance |
| MOD-HIGH | 2-3 biomarkers + strong mechanistic basis + one gap | TSH 3.2 + FT4 0.87 = likely thyroid under-function (gap: no FT3) |
| MODERATE | 1-2 biomarkers + plausible mechanism + needs confirmation | Cortisol 11 + symptoms = possible HPA dysfunction (need DUTCH) |
| LOW | Inference from indirect markers, hypothesis-stage | GGT 8 + fatigue = possible B6/Mg depletion (need direct testing) |

### 8.4 PRIORITY RANKING

Rank axes by clinical urgency:

| Priority | Criteria |
|----------|---------|
| 1 — URGENT | RED terrain markers, critical values, safety concerns |
| 2 — PRIMARY | Highest-confidence finding with most downstream impact |
| 3 — SECONDARY | Moderate-confidence findings, may resolve with Priority 2 |
| 4 — MONITOR | Low-confidence or borderline findings to watch |
| 5 — INVESTIGATE | Gaps requiring additional testing before action |

→ Output of Step 8: Clinical thesis, probability hierarchy per axis,
   confidence matrix, priority ranking.


## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## STEP 9: OUTPUT GENERATION
## Source: All previous steps → 64_DOSSIER_PROTOCOL format
## Output: Patient-facing analysis document
## ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

### 9.1 SELECT OUTPUT FORMAT

Based on request and context:

| Request | Format | Template |
|---------|--------|----------|
| "Analyze my labs" (conversation) | Conversational analysis | Steps 3-8 results in prose, dual register |
| "Write a dossier" / "Full report" | Resimont 4-layer dossier | 64_DOSSIER_PROTOCOL full structure |
| "Quick summary" | Executive summary only | Terrain + top 3 priorities + next steps |
| "Compare with previous" | Delta analysis | Prior vs current + trajectory + protocol efficacy |
| "What should I test next?" | Gap analysis | Step 1.5 gaps + Step 8 probability shifts → 36_TESTING_GUIDE |

### 9.2 DOSSIER GENERATION (Full Report)

Follow 64_DOSSIER_PROTOCOL mandatory structure:

1. **Executive Summary** — Terrain status, top 3 priorities, clinical thesis
2. **Biomarker Analysis by Axis** — Technical panel + "In Simple Terms" + connections
3. **Calculated Ratios & Indices** — All Step 4 ratios with interpretation
4. **Cross-System Patterns** — Step 7 pattern matches and connections
5. **Probability Hierarchy** — Step 8 per axis
6. **Confidence Matrix** — Step 8.3 table
7. **Protocol — Phased with Terrain Gates**
   - Phase 1: Begin Now (terrain GREEN, confidence HIGH/MOD-HIGH)
   - Phase 2: Conditional (gated behind specific tests/results)
   - Phase 3: Reassessment
8. **Missing Markers & Recommended Testing** — Step 1.5 gaps + Step 8 shifts
9. **Reassessment Timeline** — When, what to retest, expected changes
10. **Falsifiability Statement** — What we predict, when we're wrong, what to do

### 9.3 QUALITY CONTROL CHECKLIST — BEFORE DELIVERY

Run this checklist before ANY output leaves Valentine:

| # | Check | Verified? |
|---|-------|-----------|
| 1 | Every available marker has been classified (7-tier) | □ |
| 2 | All applicable ratios have been calculated | □ |
| 3 | Terrain scan completed — all 4 layers assessed | □ |
| 4 | Anomaly scan completed — 5 triggers checked | □ |
| 5 | Assay methods verified for hormones (Free T, cortisol, E2) | □ |
| 6 | Missing critical markers flagged with rationale | □ |
| 7 | No intensive protocols in Phase 1 if terrain is YELLOW/RED | □ |
| 8 | Probability hierarchy present for each non-GREEN axis | □ |
| 9 | Confidence grade assigned to each finding | □ |
| 10 | "What Would Change Our Mind" present for each axis | □ |
| 11 | Quantitative claims qualified by evidence tier | □ |
| 12 | No competitor brand names in output | □ |
| 13 | Dual register used (technical + patient-facing) | □ |
| 14 | Falsifiability statement present | □ |
| 15 | Reassessment timeline with specific markers and timepoints | □ |
| 16 | Context modifiers (meds, age, sport) applied to interpretation | □ |
| 17 | Cross-system connections mapped (no axis interpreted in isolation) | □ |
| 18 | ApoB/Lp(a) flagged if LDL >100 and not tested | □ |

**RULE: If any check fails, fix before delivering. An incomplete analysis
is worse than a delayed analysis — it creates false confidence.**


## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## ABBREVIATED WORKFLOW — QUICK REFERENCE
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

For quick reference during real-time analysis:

```
STEP 1: INTAKE
  └─ Extract values → Verify assay methods → Standardize units → Identify gaps

STEP 2: CONTEXT
  └─ Demographics → Medications → Sport/TRT → Prior labs → Modifiers

STEP 3: INDIVIDUAL SCAN (→ 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS)
  └─ Every marker → 7-tier classification → Flag extremes

STEP 4: RATIOS (→ 35_LAB_INTERPRETATION)
  └─ Calculate ALL applicable ratios → 7-tier classification → Ratio red flags

STEP 5: ANOMALIES (→ 62_CLINICAL_REASONING_ANOMALIES)
  └─ 5 triggers → Discordance pairs → Sport context

STEP 6: TERRAIN (→ 61_TERRAIN_ASSESSMENT)
  └─ 4 layers → GREEN/YELLOW/RED each → Overall = worst layer → Gating

STEP 7: SYNTHESIS (→ 35_LAB cross-system patterns + system KB files)
  └─ Identify axes → Map connections → Pattern matching

STEP 8: MODEL (→ Valentine diagnostic reasoning)
  └─ Clinical thesis → Probability hierarchy → Confidence grading → Priorities

STEP 9: OUTPUT (→ 64_DOSSIER_PROTOCOL)
  └─ Select format → Generate → Quality control checklist → Deliver
```


## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## SPECIAL WORKFLOWS
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

### WORKFLOW A: FOLLOW-UP LABS (returning patient)

Additional steps inserted between Steps 2 and 3:

**A1: DELTA ANALYSIS**
- For every marker present in both panels: calculate absolute and % change
- Flag any marker that crossed a tier boundary (e.g., SUBOPTIMAL → OPTIMAL)
- Flag any marker moving in wrong direction despite intervention
- Calculate intervention efficacy: "Omega-3 doubled → TG decreased 15%"

**A2: PROTOCOL ASSESSMENT**
- Which Phase 1 interventions were implemented?
- Which expected improvements materialized?
- Which didn't? → Reconsider hypothesis
- Are Phase 2 gates now met? → Promote to Phase 1 if yes

### WORKFLOW B: PARTIAL PANEL (limited markers)

When only a basic panel is available (<15 markers):
1. Run Steps 1-6 with available markers only
2. In Step 7, explicitly state which axes CANNOT be assessed
3. In Step 9, output format = "Preliminary Assessment" (not full dossier)
4. Primary output emphasis on Step 1.5: what additional tests are needed
5. Do NOT generate a confidence matrix — insufficient data

### WORKFLOW C: SPORT / PERFORMANCE CONTEXT

When patient is confirmed athlete, bodybuilder, or on TRT/AAS:
1. Load 63_SPORT_PERFORMANCE_CONTEXT at Step 2
2. At Step 3: apply sport-adjusted ranges for CPK, Hct, AST, LH/FSH
3. At Step 5: suppress anomaly flags for expected sport-related elevations
4. At Step 6: use sport-adjusted terrain thresholds (Hct monitoring protocol)
5. At Step 9: include TRT monitoring section if applicable (Hct protocol, E2 management)

### WORKFLOW D: FEMALE CYCLE-DEPENDENT

When patient is pre-menopausal female:
1. At Step 2: confirm cycle day or phase (follicular, ovulatory, luteal)
2. At Step 3: apply PHASE-SPECIFIC ranges for E2, P4, FSH, LH
3. Mid-luteal P4 <10 = luteal phase defect — flag
4. LH:FSH >2:1 = PCOS pattern — flag
5. If cycle phase unknown: flag "hormone interpretation unreliable without
   cycle timing" and recommend mid-luteal repeat


## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
## ANTI-PATTERNS — WHAT THIS ENGINE PREVENTS
## ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

| Without Engine | With Engine |
|---------------|-------------|
| Valentine calculates some ratios but forgets others | ALL applicable ratios calculated (Step 4 checklist) |
| Valentine interprets Free T without checking assay type | Assay verification MANDATORY before interpretation (Step 1.3) |
| Valentine misses thyroid-lipid connection | Cross-system synthesis REQUIRED (Step 7) |
| Valentine recommends NAD+ with RED terrain | Terrain gating ENFORCED (Step 6) |
| Valentine gives analysis without confidence grades | Confidence matrix MANDATORY (Step 8.3) |
| Valentine provides analysis with no falsifiability | Falsifiability REQUIRED (Step 8.2) |
| Valentine forgets to flag missing ApoB when LDL elevated | Gap identification MANDATORY (Step 1.5) |
| Valentine interprets axes in isolation | Connection mapping REQUIRED (Step 7.3) |
| Valentine inconsistency between analyses | Same 9-step workflow EVERY TIME |
| Valentine skips anomaly detection | Anomaly scan MANDATORY (Step 5) |
| Valentine forgets GGT dual interpretation | 65_ATLAS referenced systematically (Step 3) |
| Valentine gives partial analysis without flagging it | Panel level classification REQUIRED (Step 1.5) |

 === SECTION 3: PHARMACOKINETICS & DYNAMICS (ADME) [INTERNAL_ONLY] ===
## 9-STEP WORKFLOW SUMMARY

1. INTAKE — Extract, verify assays, standardize units, identify gaps
2. CONTEXT — Demographics, meds, sport, prior labs, modifiers
3. SCAN — Every marker through 65_ATLAS → 7-tier
4. RATIOS — All applicable via 35_LAB → 7-tier
5. ANOMALIES — 5 triggers + discordance via 62_CLINICAL_REASONING
6. TERRAIN — 4 layers via 61_TERRAIN → gating
7. SYNTHESIS — Axes + patterns + connections via system files
8. MODEL — Thesis + hierarchy + confidence + priorities
9. OUTPUT — Format via 64_DOSSIER → quality control → deliver

## FILE ROUTING MAP

| I need to... | Go to... |
|-------------|---------|
| Look up what a single marker means | 65_BIOMARKER_ATLAS |
| Calculate a ratio or find its ranges | 35_LAB_INTERPRETATION |
| Check if terrain allows a protocol | 61_TERRAIN_ASSESSMENT |
| Handle an unexpected lab value | 62_CLINICAL_REASONING_ANOMALIES |
| Format the final report | 64_DOSSIER_PROTOCOL |
| Figure out what to test next | 36_TESTING_GUIDE |
| Deep dive into a system mechanism | System file (06, 09, 17, 18, 19, 57…) |
| Adjust for athlete/TRT context | 63_SPORT_PERFORMANCE_CONTEXT |
| Check drug-supplement interactions | 54_DRUG_INTERACTIONS |
| Identify red flags / emergencies | 55_RED_FLAGS |
